content.problemStatement:
Go ahead and practice another problem.


Given an integer NNN - print a number pattern of height NNN. 

Check the sample output given below.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print N lines. On the i‑th line (1‑based) print the numbers 1 through i separated by a single space and ending with a trailing space. Print a newline after each line. Test cases are printed consecutively without extra blank lines.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1, N=5. The pattern prints lines from 1 up to 5, each line i containing numbers 1..i.
Only one line is printed because N=1.
First test case N=2 prints two lines. Second test case N=3 prints three lines. They are output consecutively.

solution.approach: For each test case read N. Use two nested loops: the outer loop runs i from 1 to N, the inner loop runs j from 1 to i and prints j followed by a space. After the inner loop print a newline. This directly builds the required pattern. The algorithm is optimal because every printed number must appear in the output.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) – each test case prints 1 + 2 + … + N = N(N+1)/2 numbers.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few loop counters are used.
